title: Today's Tech Brief: How small businesses can leverage AI
summary: A look at how small businesses can use AI, and the state of the AI bubble.
imageAlt: Image from source article for How small businesses can leverage AI
imageCaption: How small businesses can leverage AI
imageSourceTitle: How small businesses can leverage AI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I'm looking at some interesting developments in the world of AI, and how small businesses can leverage this technology. According to MIT Technology Review, small businesses can apply large language models, or LLMs, across various industries, from accounting to design to market research.

The article explains that LLMs can help small businesses automate tasks, gain insights from data, and even create content. For instance, LLMs can help with bookkeeping, invoicing, and customer service. My read is that this technology has the potential to level the playing field for small businesses, allowing them to compete with larger companies.

On the other hand, Axios is reporting on the 'revenge of the AI bubble.' It seems that the hype around AI is still alive and well, but some experts are warning that the bubble may burst soon. I find it interesting that there's still a lot of excitement around AI, despite concerns about its practical applications.

It's a bit like the emperor's new clothes – everyone's talking about AI, but is it really delivering on its promises? I'm not sure, but it's definitely worth keeping an eye on. As someone who's been following this space, I have to say that it's been a wild ride so far.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
